title: Risk Treatment Options
description: Four standard risk treatment strategies with BNM RMiT-specific examples

options.description: Implement controls to reduce likelihood or impact to acceptable levels
options.when: Risk is above appetite but can be reduced through controls
examples: Deploy MFA and PAM to reduce unauthorized access risk
Implement real-time transaction monitoring for fraud detection
Establish automated patching to address vulnerability window
options.rmitAlignment: S10 Cybersecurity Controls, S11 Technology Operations

options.description: Shift risk to a third party through insurance or outsourcing
options.when: Risk impact is high but likelihood can be financially offset
examples: Cyber insurance for data breach liability
Outsource DDoS protection to specialized provider
Cloud provider SLA with financial penalties for downtime

options.description: Acknowledge and retain the risk within approved appetite
options.when: Cost of treatment exceeds potential loss, or risk is within appetite
examples: Accept minor UI bugs in non-critical internal tools
Accept limited legacy system risk with compensating controls and EOL plan
Accept residual risk of sophisticated nation-state attacks
options.rmitAlignment: S9 Technology Risk Management — requires CTRO approval and Board reporting

options.description: Eliminate the risk by removing the activity or technology
options.when: Risk is unacceptable and no adequate treatment exists
examples: Discontinue use of end-of-life operating systems
options.rmitAlignment: S14 Technology Audit, S9 Risk Appetite
